review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to boundary protection. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but minor improvements can be made for clarity. The functions are technically correct and align with the intent of the control SC-7(27).
naming_improvements: Consider using 'k8s_service_nodeport_disabled' instead of 'k8s_service_type_not_nodeport' for consistency with naming patterns that indicate a clear pass/fail condition.
title: Boundary Protection - Boundary protection | unclassified non - national security system connections pr
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7(27) focuses on boundary protection, which in Kubernetes can be addressed through network policies and service configurations. NetworkPolicies can ensure that ingress and egress traffic is restricted, preventing unauthorized access. Additionally, avoiding NodePort services and ensuring TLS is enabled for Ingress controllers helps protect the boundary of the cluster. These measures align with the control's intent to protect unclassified non-national security system connections.
